```{r}
#attempt to add download button using just shiny widgets to save table displayed with Kable
# downloadButton(outputId = "saveCSV", label = "CSV")

```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| context: server

library(dplyr)
library(kableExtra)
library(DT)

#ALERT: TOGGLE CHARGE TO CASE (dispo_charge_probation_stats_1C_trial_week_ to dispo_casee_probation_stats_1C_trial_week_)

weekly_probation_table <- reactive({
    csv_selected <- list.files("WeeklyData/", pattern = paste0("^dispo_charge_probation_stats_1C_trial_week_", input$selectWeekDispo_Probation,".*?csv$"))
  dispo_trial_table_full <- read.csv(file = paste0("WeeklyData/", csv_selected))
  dispo_trial_table_full %>% rename(
    `First Revocation` = FirstRevocation,
    `Second Revocation` = SecondRevocation,
    `> 2 Revocations` = MoreThanTwo)
  })

output$probation_table <- renderDT(
  weekly_probation_table(), 
  rownames = F,
  # colnames = c("Trial week", "1C01 (Wong)", "1C02 (Remigio)", "1C03 (Ho)", "1C11 (Souza)", "1C15 (Malinao)", "1C17 (Johnson)", "1C19 (To'oto'o)", "1C20 (Morikawa)", "1C22 (Park)", "1C23 (Somerville)", "Total"),
    # colnames = c("Judge\n","Division","JRT","JWT","COP","DSM","DWO","DPJ","NPQ","RMD","MISC","Total"),
    extensions = c("Buttons", "FixedColumns"),
    options = list(
      pageLength = 15,
      # lengthMenu = c(15, 25, 50),
      # scrollY = "600px", #this was creating some weird behavior in which the column heads of previous table were partially appearing
      scrollX = TRUE,
      #add buttons, and specifying dom
      #https://stackoverflow.com/questions/50039186/add-download-buttons-in-dtrenderdatatable
      #https://stackoverflow.com/questions/50043152/r-shiny-how-to-add-pagination-in-dtrenderdatatable
      # dom = 'Bfrtip',
      dom = 'tB',
      buttons = c('copy','csv','excel','pdf'),
      fixedColumns = list(leftColumns = 1)  # Freeze first column
      # h/t: customize font size of column heads: https://github.com/rstudio/DT/issues/783
      # headerCallback = DT::JS(
      #   "function(thead) {",
      #   "  $(thead).css('font-size', '14px');",
      #   "}"
      #   )
    )
)

```

## Weekly and Cumulative Probation Dispositions

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

#ALERT: TOGGLE CHARGE TO CASE (dispo_charge_probation_stats_1C_trial_week_ to dispo_casee_probation_stats_1C_trial_week_)
csv_charge_probation_list <- list.files("WeeklyData/", pattern = "^dispo_charge_probation_stats_1C_trial_week_.*csv$")
csv_charge_probation_list <- csv_charge_probation_list[-length(csv_charge_probation_list)]

charge_probation_list <- lapply(paste0("WeeklyData/", csv_charge_probation_list), read.csv)
# h/t: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75190497/add-filename-as-an-attribute-while-reading-multiple-csv-w-lapply-or-add-filena
charge_probation_df <- bind_rows(setNames(charge_probation_list, basename(csv_charge_probation_list)), .id = "id") #bind_rows(case_dispo_list)

  charge_probation_cumulative <- charge_probation_df %>% 
    mutate(trial_week = as.Date(str_extract(id, "[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}")),
           cyear = format(trial_week, "%Y"),
           Division = if_else(Division == "-", "All Divisions", Division),
           Division = factor(Division, levels = c("1", "2", "6", "11", "13", "15", "17", "19", "20", "21",  "23", "All Divisions"),
                             labels = c("1C01 (Wong)", "1C02 (Remigio)", "1C06 (Costa)", "1C11 (Souza)", "1C13 (Viola)", "1C15 (Malinao)", "1C17 (Johnson)", "1C19 (To'oto'o)", "1C20 (Morikawa)", "1C21 (Kawashima)", "1C23 (Somerville)", "All Divisions")),
           `Weekly Probation Dispositions` = Revocations + Dismissals   
    ) %>% 
    group_by(Division, cyear) %>%   
    mutate(`Total Dispositions` = cumsum(Total),
           `Revocations` = cumsum(Revocations),
           `Dismissals` = cumsum(Dismissals)) %>% 
    ungroup()  
  
case_probation_aggregate_long <- charge_probation_cumulative %>% pivot_longer(cols = c(Revocations,`Weekly Probation Dispositions`,`Total Dispositions`), names_to = "Type", values_to = "Probation Dispositions")

case_probation_aggregate_long$Type <- factor(case_probation_aggregate_long$Type, levels = c("Total Dispositions", "Revocations", "Weekly Probation Dispositions"))

cumulative_probation_line <- subset(case_probation_aggregate_long, Type != "Weekly Probation Dispositions") %>% filter(Division == "All Divisions")
weekly_probation_bar <- subset(case_probation_aggregate_long, Type == "Weekly Probation Dispositions") %>% filter(Division == "All Divisions")

gg_probation_all <- ggplot(cumulative_probation_line %>% filter(Division == "All Divisions"),
                          aes(x = trial_week, y = `Probation Dispositions`, colour = Type, group = Type,
                              text = paste0("week: ",trial_week,"\n",
                                            "# of dispositions: ", `Probation Dispositions`, "\n",
                                            "division: ", Division, "\n",
                                            "year: ", cyear))) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_col(data = weekly_probation_bar,fill = "#FFFFFF", aes(x = trial_week, y = `Probation Dispositions`, group =  Type)) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#0000FF","#FF0000", "#000000")) +
  scale_x_date(breaks = seq(as.Date("2024-01-01"), max(charge_probation_cumulative$trial_week), by = 7), date_labels = "%m-%d-%y") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,NA)) +
  labs(title = "Weekly and Cumulative Probation Revocations/Dismissals (All Divisions)",
       x = NULL,
       y = "Number of dispositions",
       color = NULL) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30))+
  NULL

ggplotly(gg_probation_all, tooltip = "text", dynamicTicks = TRUE) %>%
      layout(
        title = list(text=paste0('Weekly and Cumulative Probation Dispos (All Divisions)')
                     ),
        margin = list(t = 90),
        xaxis = list(type = 'date',
                     autorange = FALSE,
                     rangeselector = list(
                       buttons = list(
                          list(count=3, label="3m", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                         list(count=6, label="6m", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                         list(count=1, label="YTD", step="year", stepmode="todate"),
                         list(count=1, label="1y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                         list(count=2, label="2y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                         list(step="all")
                       )
                     ),
                     #for dates on x-axis, this worked: tickmode = linear and set both tick0/dtick
                     #for % on y-axis, this worked: tickmode = array and set both tickvals/ticktext
                     #h/t: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/68475195/dticks-and-plotly-how-to-control-what-is-shown-on-x-axes
                     tickmode = 'linear', 
                     tick0 = plotStartRange,
                     dtick = 'M1',
                     tickformat = '%b %Y' #added this b/c tick labels formatted with ggplot were being cutoff by graph, this allows correct alignment and spacing
                     )#,   
        #to customize both range and # of tick labels, use this instead of the above
        #https://plotly.com/r/time-series/
        #h/t: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/71823559/how-to-set-custom-date-ticks-breaks-in-plotly
        # yaxis = list(range = c(-0.05,1.05), #changed from 0,1 to -.05,1.05 to add small margin/padding at ends of graph
        #              autorange = FALSE,
        #              tickmode = 'array',
        #              tickvals = seq(0,1,.1),
        #              ticktext = paste0(seq(0,100,10),'%'))
             ) %>%
      # rangeslider(type = 'date', start = min(case_dispo_df2$trial_week) - 7, end = max(case_dispo_df2$trial_week) + 7, thickness = .1) %>% # use +/- 7 to add some padding to left and right side of plot and rangeslider
  rangeslider(type = 'date', start = plotStartRange, end = plotEndRange, thickness = .1) %>%
  config(modeBarButtonsToRemove = c('hoverClosestCartesian','hoverCompareCartesian'), displaylogo = FALSE)

```

## Probation Trends By Division

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(shiny)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(plotly)

plotOutput("facet_probation", height = 1200) 

```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| context: server

#ALERT: TOGGLE CHARGE TO CASE (dispo_charge_probation_stats_1C_trial_week_ to dispo_casee_probation_stats_1C_trial_week_)
csv_charge_probation_list <- list.files("WeeklyData/", pattern = "^dispo_charge_probation_stats_1C_trial_week_.*csv$")
csv_charge_probation_list <- csv_charge_probation_list[-length(csv_charge_probation_list)]

  charge_probation_list <- lapply(paste0("WeeklyData/", csv_charge_probation_list), read.csv)
  
  case_probation_df <- bind_rows(setNames(charge_probation_list, basename(csv_charge_probation_list)), .id = "id") #bind_rows(case_dispo_list)
  
  case_probation_division <- case_probation_df %>% 
    mutate(trial_week = as.Date(str_extract(id, "[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}")),
           cyear = format(trial_week, "%Y"),
           Division = if_else(Division == "-", "All Divisions", Division),
           Division = factor(Division, levels = c("1", "2", "6", "11", "13", "15", "17", "19", "20", "21",  "23", "All Divisions"),
                             labels = c("1C01 (Wong)", "1C02 (Remigio)", "1C06 (Costa)", "1C11 (Souza)", "1C13 (Viola)", "1C15 (Malinao)", "1C17 (Johnson)", "1C19 (To'oto'o)", "1C20 (Morikawa)", "1C21 (Kawashima)", "1C23 (Somerville)", "All Divisions")),
           `Weekly Probation Dispositions` = Revocations + Dismissals   
    ) %>% 
    group_by(Division, cyear) %>%   
    mutate(`Total Dispositions` = cumsum(Total),
           `Revocations` = cumsum(Revocations),
           `Dismissals` = cumsum(Dismissals)) %>% 
    ungroup()  
  
   #df by month
    case_probation_division_month <- case_probation_division %>%
      mutate(period = floor_date(trial_week, "month")) %>%
      group_by(period, Division) %>%
      summarize(total_dispositions = sum(`Weekly Probation Dispositions`), .groups = "drop")
  
    #monthly facet graph
     # case_deferral_division_month %>% filter(Division != "All Divisions") %>% ggplot(aes(x = period, y = total_dispositions, group = Division, color = Division)) + 
     # geom_col(aes(fill = Division)) + 
     # facet_wrap(vars(class = Division)) +
     # labs(x = NULL) +
     # theme_bw() +
     # theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 25, face = "bold"), 
     #       legend.position="none",
     #       axis.text.x.bottom = element_text(size = 18),
     #       axis.text.y.left = element_text(size = 18),
     #       axis.title.y = element_text(size = 25, face = "bold")
     #       ) + 
     #   scale_x_date(breaks = "1 month", date_labels = "%b")
     # 
  
  output$facet_probation <- renderPlot({
  case_probation_division %>% filter(Division != "All Divisions") %>% ggplot(aes(x = trial_week, y = `Total Dispositions`, group = Division, color = Division)) +     
    geom_col(aes(fill = Division)) + 
    facet_wrap(vars(class = Division)) +
    labs(x = NULL) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 25, face = "bold"), 
          legend.position="none",
          axis.text.x.bottom = element_text(size = 18),
          axis.text.y.left = element_text(size = 18),
          axis.title.y = element_text(size = 25, face = "bold")
          ) #+ 
      #scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,15))
    } 
  ) 
```


## Weekly Probation Trends by Division

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(gghighlight)
library(ggtext)

choicesCaseDispoSelect <- case_dispo_df2 %>% 
  select(Division) %>% 
  distinct() %>% 
  filter(Division != "All Divisions") %>% 
  arrange(Division) %>% 
  pull()

# selectizeInput(inputId = "caseDispoSelect",
#                label = "Select Division:",
#                choices = choicesCaseDispoSelect,
#                selected = choicesCaseDispoSelect[1],
#                multiple = TRUE
#                )

selectizeInput(inputId = "caseDispoSelect",
               label = "Select Division:",
               choices = choicesCaseDispoSelect,
               selected = choicesCaseDispoSelect[1],
               multiple = FALSE
               )

plotlyOutput("gg_probation_divs", height = 600)

```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| context: server

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(tidyr)

#ALERT: TOGGLE CHARGE TO CASE (dispo_charge_probation_stats_1C_trial_week_ to dispo_casee_probation_stats_1C_trial_week_)
csv_charge_probation_list <- list.files("WeeklyData/", pattern = "^dispo_charge_probation_stats_1C_trial_week_.*csv$")
csv_charge_probation_list <- csv_charge_probation_list[-length(csv_charge_probation_list)]

charge_probation_list <- lapply(paste0("WeeklyData/", csv_charge_probation_list), read.csv)
# h/t: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75190497/add-filename-as-an-attribute-while-reading-multiple-csv-w-lapply-or-add-filena
charge_probation_df <- bind_rows(setNames(charge_probation_list, basename(csv_charge_probation_list)), .id = "id") #bind_rows(case_dispo_list)

charge_probation_division <- charge_probation_df %>% 
  mutate(trial_week = as.Date(str_extract(id, "[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}")),
         cyear = format(trial_week, "%Y"),
         Division = if_else(Division == "-", "All Divisions", Division),
         Division = factor(Division, levels = c("1", "2", "6", "11", "13", "15", "17", "19", "20", "21",  "23", "All Divisions"),
                           labels = c("1C01 (Wong)", "1C02 (Remigio)", "1C06 (Costa)", "1C11 (Souza)", "1C13 (Viola)", "1C15 (Malinao)", "1C17 (Johnson)", "1C19 (To'oto'o)", "1C20 (Morikawa)", "1C21 (Kawashima)",  "1C23 (Somerville)", "All Divisions"))) %>% 
  group_by(Division, cyear) %>% 
  mutate(`Weekly Probation Dispositions` = Total,
         `Total Revocations` = cumsum(Revocations),
         `Total Dismissals` = cumsum(Dismissals),
         `Total Probation Dispositions` = cumsum(Total)) %>%   
  ungroup()

yaxis_lims_probation <- charge_probation_division %>% 
    filter(Division != "All Divisions") %>%
    group_by(Judge) %>% 
    summarize(total_probation = sum(Total)) %>% 
    ungroup() %>%
  filter(Judge != "Total")

ymax_probation <- max(yaxis_lims_probation$total_probation) + 1

charge_probation_division_long <- charge_probation_division %>% pivot_longer(cols = c(`Weekly Probation Dispositions`, `Total Revocations`,`Total Probation Dispositions`), values_to = "Probation", names_to = "Type") 

charge_probation_division_long$Type <- factor(charge_probation_division_long$Type, levels = c("Weekly Probation Dispositions", "Total Probation Dispositions", "Total Revocations"))

charge_probation_division_line <- subset(charge_probation_division_long, Type != "Weekly Probation Dispositions") %>% filter(Division != "All Divisions")
charge_probation_division_bar <- subset(charge_probation_division_long, Type == "Weekly Probation Dispositions") %>% filter(Division != "All Divisions")

  charge_probation_df2 <- reactive({charge_probation_division_line %>% 
    group_by(Division, cyear) %>% 
    ungroup() %>%
    filter(Division %in% input$caseDispoSelect)
})

    charge_probation_bar <- reactive({charge_probation_division_bar %>% 
    group_by(Division, cyear) %>% 
    ungroup() %>%
    filter(Division %in% input$caseDispoSelect)
})

output$gg_probation_divs <- renderPlotly({
  
  gg_probation_divs <- ggplot(charge_probation_df2(),
                          aes(x = trial_week, y = Probation, group = Type, colour = Type,
                              text = paste0("week: ",trial_week,"\n",
                                            "cumulative # of deferrals:", Probation, "\n",
                                            "division: ", Division, "\n",
                                            "year: ", cyear))) +
      geom_col(charge_probation_bar(), 
             fill = "#FFFFFF", 
             mapping = aes(x = trial_week, y = Probation, group = Type, 
                           text = paste0("week: ",trial_week, "\n",
                                             "Weekly Probation: ", Probation, "\n",
                                            "division: ", Division, "\n",
                                            "year: ", cyear))) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_line() +
scale_color_manual(values=c("#000000","#0000FF",  "#FF0000")) +
    #ISSUE: the x-axis was showing an extra label on each end of plot, tried a few methods to fix this
    # scale_x_date("Week", date_breaks = "1 week") +
    # scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 week", date_labels = "%m-%d-%y") +
    scale_x_date(breaks = seq(as.Date("2024-01-01"), max(charge_deferrals_df2()$trial_week), by = 7), date_labels = "%m-%d-%y") +
    #ISSUE: for judges with 0 or few trials, the y-axis has non-integer values, looks bad, use method 1 as a fix:
    #method 0:
    # scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,NA)) +
    #method 1:
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, ymax_probation)) +
    #method 2:
    # scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, sum(case_dispo_df2()$Total) + 1, by = 5)) +
    # scale_y_continuous("Total Pending Trials") +
    # scale_color_discrete("Divisions") +
    labs(title = paste0("Weekly and Cumulative Deferrals in ", input$caseDispoSelect),
         x = NULL,
         y = "Number of Deferrals",
         color = "Divisions") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30)) +
         NULL

ggplotly(gg_probation_divs,tooltip = "text", dynamicTicks = TRUE) %>%
      # hide_legend() %>%
      layout(
        #moved title and subtitle to the shinydashboard box b/c it didn't format nicely with selectorbuttons, especially on smaller screens
        # title = list(text=paste0('Weekly and Cumulative Trials (All Divisions)')
        #              # xanchor = 'left', #reposition title
        #              # yanchor =  'top'  #reposition title
        #              ),
        margin = list(t = 90),
        xaxis = list(type = 'date',
                     autorange = FALSE,
                     # range = as.Date(c(plotStartRange,plotEndRange)),
                     rangeselector = list(
                       buttons = list(
                         list(count=3, label="3m", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                         list(count=6, label="6m", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                         list(count=1, label="YTD", step="year", stepmode="todate"),
                         list(count=1, label="1y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                         list(count=2, label="2y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                         list(step="all")
                       )#,
                       # x = 0.6,           #put rangeselector on right side
                       # xanchor = "left"   #put rangeselector on right side
                     ),
                     #for dates on x-axis, this worked: tickmode = linear and set both tick0/dtick
                     #for % on y-axis, this worked: tickmode = array and set both tickvals/ticktext
                     #h/t: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/68475195/dticks-and-plotly-how-to-control-what-is-shown-on-x-axes
                     tickmode = 'linear', 
                     tick0 = plotStartRange,
                     dtick = 'M1',
                     tickformat = '%b %Y' #added this b/c tick labels formatted with ggplot were being cutoff by graph, this allows correct alignment and spacing
                     ),   
        #to customize both range and # of tick labels, use this instead of the above
        #https://plotly.com/r/time-series/
        #h/t: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/71823559/how-to-set-custom-date-ticks-breaks-in-plotly
        # yaxis = list(range = c(-0.05,1.05), #changed from 0,1 to -.05,1.05 to add small margin/padding at ends of graph
        #              autorange = FALSE,
        #              tickmode = 'array',
        #              tickvals = seq(0,1,.1),
        #              ticktext = paste0(seq(0,100,10),'%'))
          yaxis = list(autorange = FALSE)
             ) %>%

      # rangeslider(type = 'date', start = min(case_dispo_df2()$trial_week) - 7, end = max(case_dispo_df2()$trial_week) + 7, thickness = .1) %>% # use +/- 7 to add some padding to left and right side of plot and rangeslider
      rangeslider(type = 'date', start = plotStartRange, end = plotEndRange, thickness = .1) %>%
  config(modeBarButtonsToRemove = c('hoverClosestCartesian','hoverCompareCartesian'), displaylogo = FALSE)

})
```
